channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Rec Room shutting down after failing to become 'sustainability profitable'
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

March 31, 2026

2 Min Read

VR chat and game platform Rec Room is shutting down on June 1, 2026. The closure will come precisely a decade after the title launched.

Eponymous Rec Room developer Rec Room explained it never figured out how to effectively monetize the experience, which was available on numerous VR platforms including Meta Quest, PlayStation VR, and Pico. 

The title could also be played without a VR headset on console and PC platforms, and featured a suite of UGC tools that enabled players to create their own play experiences called 'Rooms.' 

Rec Room was freely accessible but featured purchasable in-game currency in the form of tokens that could be spent on items such as cosmetics. An optional paid membership called Rec Room Plus was also introduced for $7.99/month and offered unique benefits such as discounts, exclusive items, and a monthly token allowance. 

"The top UGC rooms saw over 500 years of play time each. That’s a lot of people having a lot of fun. What this community built together is incredible, and something we'll always be proud of. Even today, millions of people are showing up to spend time in this fun and welcoming place every month," reads a blog post announcing the shutdown. 

Related:Embracer Group has laid off 124 employees at Eidos Montreal

"Despite this popularity, we never quite figured out how to make Rec Room a sustainably profitable business. Our costs always ended up overwhelming the revenue we brought in. We spent a long time trying to find a way to make the numbers work. But with the recent shift in the VR market, along with broader headwinds in gaming, the path to profitability has gotten tough enough that we've made the difficult decision to shut things down."

Rec Room previously laid off roughly half of its workforce in August 2025—again citing a need to adjust based on the "financial trajectory of the company." 

The studio raised $145 million at a valuation of $3.5 billion in 2021. It secured around $300 million in funding across its lifetime. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/rec-room-shutting-down-after-failing-to-become-sustainability-profitable-
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Embracer Group has laid off 124 employees at Eidos Montreal
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

March 31, 2026

1 Min Read

Deus Ex and Marvel's Guardians of the Galaxy developer Eidos Montreal has laid off 124 employees. Head of studio, David Anfossi, has also departed after almost two decades at the company. 

According to his LinkedIn page, Anfossi joined Eidos Montreal in April 2007 and initially served as a producer on Deus Ex Human: Revolution. He was named executive producer in 2011 before being appointed head of studio in 2013.

It is the latest in a steady stream of redundancies at the studio, which has been downsized repeatedly by parent company Embracer Group since it acquired Eidos Montreal from Square Enix in 2022. 

A company statement posted on LinkedIn said the cuts will impact workers across production and support teams and are the result of "changing project needs."

"Today is a difficult day for our studio and reflects the need to adapt and concentrate efforts where Eidos-Montréal can be most effective," it reads.

Related:PUBG Blindspot shuts down after 2 months in early access

"We are deeply grateful to the team members impacted; this decision is not a reflection of their talent, dedication, or performance. Supporting those impacted with care and respect remains our priority, while ensuring continuity for the teams moving forward."

The exact reason for Anfossi's departure is unclear. His exit is being positioned as a parting of the ways, with Eidos noting that a transition plan is currently being implemented.

Eidos Montreal previously laid off 75 employees in April 2025. The company said that round of cuts was one of its "mandates" coming to an end. It made also eliminated 97 roles in January 2024 with Embracer embarking upon a far-reaching cost reduction program.

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/embracer-has-laid-off-124-employees-at-eidos-montreal
